key_insight: The paper establishes CsPbCl3 as a thermal calibration and stability benchmark to more accurately identify true phase-transition and decomposition temperatures in halide perovskites, then applies the same approach to Cs2AgSbCl6.
engineering_relevance: This methodology improves the reliability of high-temperature phase-stability screening for perovskite materials used in optoelectronics and solar-cell development.
method: Ligand-assisted re-precipitation synthesis of CsPbCl3 and related cesium/chlorine compounds
In-situ temperature-dependent X-ray diffraction to track phase evolution
Use of a control substrate and refinements to estimate actual sample temperature
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the authors used crystalline-structure and morphology analysis plus in-situ XRD to confirm known thermal behavior, determine phase transitions, and identify decomposition onsets in the studied powders.
watch_item: Temperature measurements and inferred stability windows depend on calibration quality, so errors in sample temperature estimation or heating uniformity could affect the reported transition points.
paper_title: Thermal Corrections and Analysis on the Phase Stability of CsPbCl3 and Cs2AgSbCl6 during In-Situ Thermal Treatment
